structured.summary:
This paper introduces a novel approach to interpretability in deep neural networks.

The authors propose a method that decomposes model weights into interpretable components using sparse coding.

Experiments on standard benchmarks demonstrate that the approach achieves strong performance while maintaining interpretability.
structured.keyFindings: The proposed sparse decomposition method recovers interpretable features that correlate with human-understandable concepts across multiple architectures.
structured.methodology: The authors apply dictionary learning to intermediate layer activations, fitting a sparse overcomplete basis that minimizes reconstruction error with L1 regularization.
structured.limitations: The method requires manual inspection to label discovered features, which does not scale well to very large models with millions of parameters.
structured.relevanceAssessment: This work is highly relevant to mechanistic interpretability research and provides practical tools for understanding model internals, representing an improvement over the abstract-based score of 7.5/10.
